author: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy
book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: "In the beginning, then," he says, "these two substances were divided.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: The empire of light was held by God the Father, who is perpetual in holy origin, magnificent in virtue, true in His very nature, ever rejoicing in His own eternity, possessing in Himself wisdom and the vital senses, by which He also includes the twelve members of His light, which are the plentiful resources of his kingdom.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Also in each of His members are stored thousands of untold and priceless treasures.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: But the Father Himself, chief in praise, incomprehensible in greatness, has united to Himself happy and glorious worlds, incalculable in number and duration, along with which this holy and illustrious Father and Progenitor resides, no poverty or infirmity being admitted in His magnificent realms.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And these matchless realms are so founded on the region of light and bliss, that no one can ever move or disturb them."

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Where is the proof of all this?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And where did Manichæus learn it?

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Do not frighten me with the name of the Paraclete.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For, in the first place, I have come not to put faith in unknown things, but to get the knowledge of undoubted truths, according to the caution enjoined on me by yourselves.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For you know how bitterly you taunt those who believe without consideration.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And what is more, this writer, who here begins to tell of very doubtful things, himself promised a little before to give complete and well-grounded knowledge.
